operating_state: Total net sales increased 13% to $143.3 billion driven by AWS growth of 17%, North America segment growth of 12%, and International segment achieving positive operating income versus prior year loss. Third-party seller services and advertising services expanded 16% and 24% respectively. Operating income surged to $15.3 billion from $4.8 billion reflecting improved operational efficiency and margin expansion across segments.
cost_structure: Operating expenses increased modestly while revenue growth significantly outpaced cost increases, driving substantial operating leverage. Server depreciation expense decreased $897 million due to extending useful lives from five to six years, providing $695 million net income benefit. Fulfillment costs rose 7% while technology infrastructure costs remained flat and sales and marketing expenses declined 5% from reduced personnel and advertising costs.
material_events: Extended server useful lives from five to six years effective January 2024, reducing quarterly depreciation by $897 million. Invested additional $2.75 billion in Anthropic convertible notes bringing total investment to $4.0 billion. Jeffrey Bezos adopted Rule 10b5-1 plan to sell up to 25 million shares through December 2025. Cash capital expenditures increased to $13.9 billion primarily for AWS infrastructure and fulfillment capacity.
macro_exposures: Tax liability exposure increased as jurisdictions implement revenue-based taxes targeting online commerce, with Indian tax authorities asserting cloud services tax obligations and Luxembourg disputes regarding intangible asset distributions. Foreign exchange exposure includes currency fluctuations reducing Q1 sales by $164 million. China-based seller dependencies create vulnerability to regulatory restrictions and geopolitical tensions.
forward_outlook: Management expects Q2 2024 net sales between $144.0-149.0 billion with operating income of $10.0-14.0 billion. Cash capital expenditures expected to meaningfully increase in 2024 driven by technology infrastructure investments. AWS performance obligations for multi-year contracts totaled $157.7 billion with weighted-average remaining life of 4.1 years.
uncertainty_profile: Income tax contingencies increased to $5.5 billion with potential additional assessments across multiple jurisdictions. Patent litigation resulted in $525 million jury award against AWS services under appeal. Multiple competition and antitrust investigations by FTC and state attorneys general create regulatory overhang with potential for substantial fines or operational restrictions.
